creator: freebsd@bengrimm.net
text:
While I appreciate how inconvenient it must be, you have to realize that *all* anonymizing services, including public VPN networks, are used by script kiddies and their ilk, and that popular (well-indexed by Google) forums are among their prime targets.

The FreeBSD Forums employ blacklisting to keep them out, deriving their entries by importing listings from several sources (a broad array of unrelated firewalls, plus specific services for forums abuse, like StopForumSpam).

Every IP address that is imported will be blacklisted for thirty days, and will expire automatically thereafter.

I will not go down the entire list you provided, because I think you'll get the picture after reading the details for the first five entries:

212.84.181.72 - not currently blacklisted, but has a history:
http://stopforumspam.com/ipcheck/212.84.181.72

5.152.210.246 - imported blacklisting, expires 20170719
http://stopforumspam.com/ipcheck/5.152.210.246

5.152.210.253 - imported blacklisting, expires 20170711
http://stopforumspam.com/ipcheck/5.152.210.253

130.185.151.227 - imported blacklisting, expires 20170717
http://stopforumspam.com/ipcheck/130.185.151.227

88.150.206.163 - not currently blacklisted, but has a history:
http://stopforumspam.com/ipcheck/88.150.206.163

All of these have been listed and delisted more than once, so all are likely to be repeat offenders.

All I can do is give you this resource to look up what the problem with a specific IP address can be (though we do use other sources, most of the forum-specific hits will be found on StopForumSpam). 

Add a month to the latest entry, and you'll know whether it is (still) blacklisted.

I have no better news. 

This works extremely well for the safety and cleanliness of the FreeBSD Forums, and we plan to keep using it.

Ben a.k.a. DutchDaemon,
FreeBSD Forums Administrator.
